title: Telnet
overview: Telnet is a legacy application-layer protocol that provides command-line access to remote systems. It operates over TCP port 23 and allows users to remotely log into a system to execute commands, manage files, or perform administrative tasks.

study.overview: Telnet is a legacy application-layer protocol that provides command-line access to remote systems. It operates over TCP port 23 and allows users to remotely log into a system to execute commands, manage files, or perform administrative tasks.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

importantCommands.code:
$ telnet 192.168.1.10
Trying 192.168.1.10...
Connected to 192.168.1.10.
Escape character is '^]'.

tips: Telnet may still appear in legacy systems or embedded devices, making it important for analysts to understand how it behaves and how vulnerable it is.
